channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Justice Department directs prosecutors to probe local efforts to obstruct immigration enforcement
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department is directing its federal prosecutors to investigate any state or local officials who stand in the way of beefed-up enforcement of immigration laws under the Trump administration, according to a memo to the entire workforce obtained by The Associated Press on
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump excoriates bishop as 'Radical Left hard line Trump hater' after politically charged prayer service
item.description: Trump blasted Bishop Mariann Edgar Budde after she used her prayer service remarks to deliver left-wing remarks directed toward Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Secretary of State Marco Rubio pauses refugee operations, ramps up visa vetting
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is advising his department to cease refugee resettlement operations and begin ramping up vetting of visas from certain regions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump order puts thousands of Afghan allies waiting for US resettlement in limbo
item.description: A day-one Trump administration order puts on pause the plans of Afghan allies who have been approved for resettlement in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump targets Alaska's oil and other resources as environmentalists gear up for a fight
item.description: Alaska's political leaders are cheering an expansive executive order signed by President Donald Trump that aims to boost oil and gas drilling, mining and logging in the state.
item.dc:creator: Becky Bohrer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: GOP-led states are emboldened to keep rolling back trans rights. Democrats struggle with a response
item.description: Republicans in statehouses are emboldened by GOP electoral successes to push a new wave of bills to restrict transgender rights and gender-affirming care.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Demillo, John Hanna And Nadia Lathan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: White House OPM orders all DEI offices to begin closing by end of day Wednesday
item.description: The Office of Personnel Management tells agency and department heads they must close all DEIA offices by the end of Wednesday and put government workers in those offices on paid leave.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Career Justice Department officials reassigned to different positions: reports
item.description: The U.S. Justice Department reportedly moved several senior officials to other divisions ahead of the Senate's anticipated confirmation of President Trump's attorney general pick.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pete Hegseth’s former sister-in-law alleges abuse against second wife in affidavit to Senate
item.description: Senators vetting the nomination of Minnesotan Pete Hegseth for defense secretary received an affidavit Tuesday from a former sister-in-law alleging that the onetime Fox News host was abusive to his second wife, to the point where she feared for her safety. Hegseth denies the allegations.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Estimated 24.6 million TV viewers watched inauguration coverage, smallest audience since 2013
item.description: The Nielsen Company says an estimated 24.6 million people watched coverage of President Donald Trump's second inauguration.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Key career officials at Justice Department reassigned to different positions, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department has reassigned key senior officials across multiple divisions as part of a leadership shakeup ahead of the expected confirmation of President Donald Trump's pick for attorney general, Pam Bondi, multiple people familiar with the matter said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: U.S. throws out policies limiting arrests of migrants at sensitive locations like schools, churches
item.description: Officers enforcing immigration laws will now be able to arrest migrants at sensitive locations like schools and churches after the Trump administration threw out policies limiting where those arrests could happen.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Inauguration prayer service's pastor asks Trump to 'have mercy' on transgender kids, immigrants
item.description: “I ask you to have mercy upon the people in our country who are scared. There are gay, lesbian, transgender children, Democratic, Republican, independent families — some who fear for their lives,” she said.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Pastor Lorenzo Sewell says politics and spirituality intertwined
item.description: Sewell joined "NewsNation Now" to discuss the topic, contending there can be no spirituality without politics and vice versa.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration strips schools, churches of immigration enforcement protections
item.description: The memo rescinds a Biden administration guideline that created "protected areas" consisting of places where "children gather, disaster or emergency relief sites, and social services establishments.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo and Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: Trump’s federal health website scrubs ‘abortion’ search results
item.description: The most “relevant” results that come up in a search of “abortion” on HHS.gov, the website for the federal Department of Health and Human Services, are several years old, from the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota and other states sue to stop Trump’s order blocking birthright citizenship
item.description: Attorneys general from 22 states have sued to block President Donald Trump’s move to end a century-old immigration policy known as birthright citizenship guaranteeing that U.S.-born children are citizens regardless of their parents’ status.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump's first day back in White House includes firings, infrastructure announcement
item.description: President Donald Trump is spending his first full day back in the White House meeting with congressional leaders, making an infrastructure announcement and demonstrating one of his favored expressions of power: firing people.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump demands an apology from bishop who asked him to ‘have mercy’ on LGBTQ+ people and migrants
item.description: President Donald Trump is demanding an apology from the Episcopal bishop of Washington after she asked him to have mercy on the LGBTQ+ community and migrants in the United States illegally during a prayer service marking his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Tiffany Stanley And Gary Fields, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: President Trump urged to pardon 76-year-old grandmother, others in prison for pro-life activism
item.description: Anti-abortion groups are calling on the president to pardon a 76-year-old grandmother and 20 others who were imprisoned and prosecuted for pro-life protests under the Biden Department of Justice.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here's what the Paris climate agreement does and doesn't do
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The 2015 Paris climate agreement is not the boogeyman that punishes the United States that critics such as President Donald Trump claim. But it hasn't quite kept the world from overheating either.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 4 states sue to block illegal migrants from census count used to assign congressional seats, electoral votes
item.description: Louisiana, Ohio, Kansas and West Virginia sued to block illegal immigrants from being counted when apportioning congressional seats and electoral votes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democratic AGs sue Trump over birthright citizenship executive order
item.description: Twenty-two Democrat-led states and two cities challenged President Trump’s executive order restricting birthright citizenship, that on Tuesday kicked off the first legal battles between his new Justice Department and Democratic attorneys general.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia warns Trump against seizing Panama Canal
item.description: Russian officials reaffirmed their commitment to the neutrality of the critical trade route and said it expects both America and Panama to continue respecting that agreement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration ousts Linda Fagan, first female Coast Guard commandant
item.description: Adm. Kevin Lunday, the former vice commandant of the Coast Guard, will take over as acting commandant, the statement notes, offering no other details of the switch.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Cannon blocks DOJ from sharing Smith's Mar-a-Lago report with lawmakers
item.description: U.S. District Judge Aileen Cannon blocked the Justice Department from sharing with four top lawmakers the volume of former special counsel Jack Smith’s report reviewing his investigation into President Trump’s mishandling of classified records.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Court cases could shape
item.description: The Minnesota Supreme Court will hear arguments this week over whether the Republican-only start to session in the House is lawful. Two court decisions late last week have also set the stage for more partisan standoffs.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump orders end of humanitarian parole for migrants of four countries
item.description: Under the Biden administration, migrants from embattled countries could apply for entry due for humanitarian reasons, without having to attempt to cross into the U.S. illegally.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: Trump declares energy emergency
item.description: President Trump has declared an energy emergency, which his team has said would unlock additional powers to jumpstart production.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Senate confirms Marco Rubio as secretary of state, giving Trump the first member of his Cabinet
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate quickly confirmed Marco Rubio as secretary of state Monday, voting unanimously to give President Donald Trump the first member of his new Cabinet on Inauguration Day.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What is an executive order? A look at Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: An incoming president signing a flurry of executive orders is standard practice. Executive orders allow a president to wield power without action from Congress. But there are also limits to what orders can achieve.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Special session rebuttal on illegal immigration continues in Florida
item.description: The governor gave his order Monday with House and Senate leadership pushing back, and that's not the only rebuttal we've seen in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gavel in or gavel out? DeSantis calls for amendment reform
item.description: There are still a lot of questions about Governor Ron DeSantis' call for a special session in less than two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pam Bondi grilled over Trump's influence at AG hearing: 5 takeaways
item.description: Pam Bondi, President-elect Trump’s pick to lead the Department of Justice, echoed attacks on the agency she would lead if confirmed, but demurred on how she might handle tests of its independence. Bondi is a more traditional pick to lead the department than Trump’s initial choice, former Rep.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC
